LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER SEIFER, MURKEN, DESPINA, JAMES & TEICHMAN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. PURSUANT TO CCP 473 (A), PETITIONER'S MOTION TO AMEND IS GRANTED. CCP 473(A) IS TO BE CONSTRUED LIBERALLY TO ALLOW "AN AMENDMENT TO ANY PLEADING OR PROCEEDING." RESPONDENT WILL NOT BE PREJUDICED BY THE AMENDMENT. THE AMENDED PLEADING MUST BE FILED WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER SEIFER, MURKEN, DESPINA, JAMES & TEICHMAN'S MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF ARBITRATOR (CCP 1281.6) IS DENIED. THE PARTIES AGREED TO BINDING ARBITRATION WITH THE BAR ASSOCIATION OF SAN FRANCISCO ("BASF"). THIS AGREEMENT IS UNENFORCEABLE UNDER THE BASF RULES AND THE MANDATORY FEE ARBITRATION ACT. SEE BENJAMIN, WEILL, & MAZER V. KORS (2011) 195 CAL.APP.4TH 40, 54. THE COURT CANNOT NOW APPOINT AN ARBITRATOR OR COMPEL BINDING ARBITRATION IN A FORUM WHICH DIFFERS FROM THE ONE SELECTED BY THE PARTIES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DECLARATION OF RESPONDENT'S COUNSEL IN SUPPORT OF SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOS TO PETITIONERS MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED PETITION TO COMPEL FILED BY RESPONDENT HYAMS, ALAN

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT ALAN HYAMS' HEARING ON DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION/COMPLAINT IN QUANTUM MERUIT AND TO ENFORCE LIEN IS OVERRULED. WEISS V. MARCUS REJECTED THE CONTENTION THAT FRACASSE V. BRENT ((1972) 6 CAL.3D 784) ABROGATED THE RULE THAT AN ATTORNEY WHO HAS BEEN DISCHARGED IS ENTITLED TO ENFORCE HIS LIEN TO THE EXTENT OF THE REASONABLE VALUE OF HIS SERVICES PRIOR TO BEING DISCHARGED. ((1975) 51 CAL.APP.3D 590.) WEISS HELD THAT A DISCHARGED ATTORNEY MAY RECOVER THE QUANTUM MERUIT VALUE OF HIS SERVICES BY ENFORCING A LIEN ESTABLISHED IN A WRITTEN AGREEMENT. (ID.) CAL. CODE OF CIV. PROC. 337 PROVIDES THAT "AN ACTION UPON ANY CONTRACT, OBLIGATION, OR LIABILITY FOUNDED UPON AN INSTRUMENT IN WRITING'" HAS A STATUTE OF LIMITATION OF FOUR YEARS. THE LIABILITY ALLEGED IS FOUNDED UPON AN INSTRUMENT IN WRITING AS PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO ENFORCE THE LIEN PROVISION IN THE WRITTEN CONTINGENCY FEE AGREEMENT. (AMENDED PETITION/COMPLAINT, 6, "PETITIONER, THEREFORE, AMENDS THIS PLEADING TO PROCEED IN THIS COURT ACTION AGAINST RESPONDENT TO RECOVER ON ITS UNDERLYING CLAIM, TO WIT, AN ACTION TO ENFORCE ITS LIEN AND TO RECOVER IN QUANTUM MERUIT FOR THE VALUE OF THE SERVICES IT RENDERED AND DOES HEREBY ASSERT THOSE CLAIMS.") ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
ORDER ON RESPONDENT/DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER

Notice Of Hearing On Demurrer To Amended Petition To Compel Arbitration/Complaint In Quantum Meruit And To Enforce Lien
Off Calendar Dec-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARTIN GLICKFELD, ESQ., 415.441.7491, FOR PETITIONER SEIFER, MURKEN, DESPINA, JAMES & TEICHMAN.
GARY BOLINSKI, ESQ., 650.367.6811, FOR RESPONDENT ALAN HYAMS.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: RESPONDENT ALAN HYAMS' Hearing On Demurrer To Amended Petition To Compel Arbitration/Complaint In Quantum Meruit And To Enforce Lien IS Overruled. Weiss v. Marcus rejected the contention that Fracasse v. Brent ((1972) 6 Cal.3d 784) abrogated the rule that an attorney who has been discharged is entitled to enforce his lien to the extent of the reasonable value of his services prior to being discharged. ((1975) 51 Cal.App.3d 590.) Weiss held that a discharged attorney may recover the quantum meruit value of his services by enforcing a lien established in a written agreement. (Id.) Cal. Code of Civ. Proc. 337 provides that "an action upon any contract, obligation, or liability founded upon an instrument in writing'" has a statute of limitation of four years. The liability alleged is founded upon an instrument in writing as plaintiff seeks to enforce the lien provision in the written contingency fee agreement. (Amended Petition/Complaint, 6, "Petitioner, therefore, amends this pleading to proceed in this Court action against Respondent to recover on its underlying claim, to wit, an action to enforce its lien and to recover in quantum meruit for the value of the services it rendered and does hereby assert those claims.") Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Notice Of Motiona Dnm Otion For Appointment Of Arbitrator; Ccp 1281.6
Off Calendar May-31-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Martin Glickfield, Esq. of the Law Office of Martin Glickfield, Tel. 415.441.7491, for petitioner and moving party;
Gregory C. Cattermole, Esq. of Carcione, Cattermole, Dolinski, Stucky, Markowitz & Carcione, Tel. 650.367.6811, for respondent and opposing party Mr. Allan Hyams, who was on Court Call.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER SEIFER, MURKEN, DESPINA, JAMES & TEICHMAN'S Motion For Appointment Of Arbitrator (CCP 1281.6) is denied. The parties agreed to binding arbitration with the Bar Association of San Francisco ("BASF"). This agreement is unenforceable under the BASF rules and the Mandatory Fee Arbitration Act. See Benjamin, Weill, & Mazer v. Kors (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 40, 54. The Court cannot now appoint an arbitrator or compel binding arbitration in a forum which differs from the one selected by the parties. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
